mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls raises aggregate wage income and consumer spending, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward hit rate of positive 20-day returns following payroll growth is below 52%, or Rank IC is negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 0.25% increases the risk-free discount rate and corporate borrowing costs, which reduces present equity values and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the unemployment rate and consumer price index over a three-month window signal stagflation, which compresses corporate margins and risk appetite, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances over a walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery, a sustained increase in housing starts signals durable demand normalization and construction multiplier effects, which drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In identified post-crisis recovery periods, HOUST acceleration is followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates accelerating manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts near-term earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances fail to produce positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales reflects robust consumer expenditure and nominal revenue expansion for listed corporations, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between RSAFS growth and 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals tightening labor markets and accelerating household income, which lifts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rapid unemployment declines coincide with negative 5-day returns in more than 45% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in the federal funds rate and consumer prices within the same month indicate aggressive monetary tightening into an inflationary economy, which raises discount rates and input costs simultaneously, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of walk-forward instances.
